sections.content: World model: Clicking M square at (39,36) changed board but counts unchanged (M:5, p:1, Y:2, S:1). Clicking M squares on board doesn't convert them. Need different approach. Let me check what actually changed and try clicking the blob or the remaining S dot.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed by comparing before/after
# First, let's see the current M square positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
m_squares = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M']
print("Current M squares:")
for n in m_squares:
    print(f"  {n['boundary']}")

# Check the blob
blob = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
if blob:
    print(f"Blob: {blob[0]['pixels']} pixels, boundary={blob[0]['boundary'][:4]}...")

# Check all nodes to see what changed
print("\nAll nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:  # Only large objects
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']} pixels, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}...")

# Try clicking the remaining S dot at (1,1) to see if it does something
# Actually, let's try clicking the blob first
blob_center = None
if blob:
    bc = blob[0]['boundary']
    blob_center = (sum(p[0] for p in bc)//len(bc), sum(p[1] for p in bc)//len(bc))
    print(f"\nBlob center: {blob_center}")
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': blob_center[0], 'col': blob_center[1]}])
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    m2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M']
    p2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
    y2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']
    s2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S' and n['pixels'] <= 4]
    blob2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
    print(f"\nAfter clicking blob: M={len(m2)}, p={len(p2)}, Y={len(y2)}, S={len(s2)}")
    if blob2:
        print(f"Blob: {blob2[0]['pixels']}")
    print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
